content.full_text:
STATE OF CALIFORNIA 
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION 
• 428 J Street • Suite 620 • Sacramento, CA 95814-2329 
(916) 322-5660 • Fax (916) 322-0886 
Debbie Peterson 
Grover Beach City Councilmember 
154 S. 8th Street 
Grover Beach, CA 93433 
Re: 
Your Request for Advice 
Our File No. A-17-111 
Dear Ms. Peterson: 
May 16, 2017 
This letter responds to your request for advice regarding the conflict of interest provisions of 
the Political Reform Act (the "Act").1 Please note that this advice does not analyze any other area of 
law, including Section 1090, the Public Contract Code, or common law conflicts of interest. Please 
also note that we are not a finder of fact when rendering advice (In re Oglesby (1975) 1 FPPC Ops. 
71 ), and any advice we provide assumes your facts are complete and accurate. 
QUESTION 
Does the Act prohibit you from taking part in decisions relating to a land use ordinance that 
will determine the zone within the City in which commercial medical marijuana businesses will be 
permitted, given that you are a full-time real estate broker for Peterson Team Realty and that your 
real estate office is within 500 feet of the proposed medical marijuana zone? 
CONCLUSION 
Yes. The Act prohibits you from taking part in decisions relating to the land use ordinance 
because those decisions will have a reasonably foreseeable material financial effect on your 
business interest in Peterson Team Realty. 
FACTS 
The Grover Beach City Council will soon consider a land use ordinance that will determine 
the zone within the City in which commercial medical marijuana businesses will be permitted. You 
are a City Councilmember and a full-time real estate broker for Peterson Team Realty. You own a 
mixed-use building that contains both the Peterson Team Realty real estate office and your personal 
residence. The building is located outside, but within 500 feet, of the proposed commercial medical 
1 The Political Refonn Act is contained in Government Code Sections 81000 through 91014. All statutory 
references are to the Government Code, unless otherwise indicated. The regulations of the Fair Political Practices 
Commission are contained in Sections 18110 through 18997 of Title 2 of the California Code of Regulations. All 
regulatory references are to Title 2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations, unless otherwise indicated. 


File No. A-17-111 
Page No. 2 
marijuana zone. Peterson Team Realty's website is "www.debbiepeterson.com," and the following 
text is prominently displayed at the top of that website's homepage: 
"NEED INFORMATION ON THE CANNABIS ORDINANCES IN 
GROVER BEACH? MY OFFICE IS LOCATED RIGHT IN THE MIDST OF 
ALL THE ACTIVITY AND I AM THE LEADING EXPERT IN THE AREA. 
CONTACT ME FOR A COPY OF THE PETERSON TEAM REAL TY 
ECONOMIC IMPACT REPORT OR FOR THE LA TEST UPDATES." 
You state that real estate prices in the proposed commercial medical marijuana zone have 
increased significantly, and that you have received inquiries from existing and potential clients who 
are considering buying, selling, or leasing real estate in the City because it will be the first in San 
Luis Obispo County to permit commercial medical marijuana businesses. 
ANALYSIS 
Section 87100 prohibits any public official from making, participating in the making, or 
otherwise using his or her official position to influence a governmental decision in which the 
official has a financial interest. Section 87103 provides that an official has a "financial interest" in a 
decision, within the meaning of the Act, if it is reasonably foreseeable that the decision will have 
material financial effect on one or more of the official's interests, identified in that section. Of the 
interests identified in Section 8 7103, the decisions at issue and the facts presented implicate the 
following: 
• An interest in any business entity in which the official has invested $2,000 or more, or in which 
the official is a director, officer, partner, trustee, employee, or holds any position of 
management. (Section 87103(a) and (d).) Assuming you have invested $2,000 or more, or hold 
a management position, in Peterson Team Realty, you have an interest in that business entity. 
• An interest in any real property in which the public official has a direct or indirect investment 
worth $2,000 or more. (Section 87103(b).) Assuming you have invested $2,000 or more in your 
mixed-use property, you have an interest in that real property. 
• An interest in any source of income aggregating $500 or more provided to, received by, or 
promised to the official within 12 months of the decision. (Section 87103(c).) Pursuant to 
Regulation I 8700.l(c), an official who receives commission income as a broker, agent, or 
salesperson for a specific sale or similar transaction may have interests in both the person the 
agent represents and the business entity under whose auspices he or she works. Thus, both 
Peterson Team Realty and any other person from whom you receive commission income are 
sources of income to you if your commission income from that person aggregates to $500 or 
more in the 12 months prior to a decision. 
• An interest in the official's personal finances or those of an immediate family member. (See 
Section 87103.) An official always has an interest in his or her personal finances. 


Foreseeability and Materiality 
File No. A-17-111 
Page No. 3 
Regulation 18701(a) provides that a decision's effect on an official's interest is presumed to 
be reasonably foreseeable if the interest is a named party in, or the subject of the decision; and an 
interest is the subject of a proceeding if the decision involves the issuance, renewal, approval, 
denial, or revocation of any license, pennit, or other entitlement to, or contract with, the interest. If 
the official is not explicitly involved in the decision, Regulation 18701(b) provides that the 
decision's effect on the interest is reasonably foreseeable if it can be recognized as a realistic 
possibility and more than hypothetical or theoretical. 
None of your interests are explicitly involved the decisions relating to the land use 
ordinance. Therefore, the effect of those decisions on your interests is reasonably foreseeable if it 
can be recognized as a realistic possibility and more than hypothetical or theoretical. 
Regulation 18702.l(b) provides that a decision's effect is material if a prudent person with 
sufficient infonnation would find it is reasonably foreseeable that the decision's effect would 
contribute to a change in the value of a privately-held business entity. That regulation also provides 
various examples of the type of decisions that may impact the analysis, including the example of 
decisions that would increase or decrease the need for products or services that the business entity 
supplies. (Regulation 18 702. I (b )(3 ). ) 
You stated that real estate prices in the proposed commercial medical marijuana zone have 
increased significantly, and that you have received inquiries from existing and potential clients who 
are considering buying, selling, or leasing real estate in the City because it will be the first in the 
County to permit commercial medical marijuana businesses. Furthermore, Peterson Team Realty's 
website prominently features the City's cannabis ordinances, advertises you as a leading expert on 
those ordinances, and indicates that your office is "right in the midst of all the activity." Therefore, 
we find that the decisions relating to the land use ordinance would contribute to a change in the 
value of Peterson Team Realty by increasing demand for its services. 
Accordingly, we conclude that the Act would prohibit you from taking part in decisions 
relating to the land use ordinance because those decisions would have a reasonably foreseeable 
material financial effect on your business interest in Peterson Team Realty.2 
2 Because we have already determined that the decisions at issue would have a reasonably foreseeable material 
financial effect on your business interest in Peterson Team Realty, we do not further analyze those decisions' effect on 
your other interests. 


File No. A-17-111 
Page No. 4 
If you have other questions on this matter, please contact me at (916) 322-5660. 
By: 
MFC:jgl 
Sincerely, 
Hyla P. Wagner 
General Counsel 
Matlhew F. Chris> 
Counsel, Legal Division

sections.analysis:
Section 87100 prohibits any public official from making, participating in the making, or 
otherwise using his or her official position to influence a governmental decision in which the 
official has a financial interest. Section 87103 provides that an official has a "financial interest" in a 
decision, within the meaning of the Act, if it is reasonably foreseeable that the decision will have

Regulation 18701(a) provides that a decision's effect on an official's interest is presumed to

None of your interests are explicitly involved the decisions relating to the land use

Regulation 18702.l(b) provides that a decision's effect is material if a prudent person with

You stated that real estate prices in the proposed commercial medical marijuana zone have

Accordingly, we conclude that the Act would prohibit you from taking part in decisions 
relating to the land use ordinance because those decisions would have a reasonably foreseeable 
material financial effect on your business interest in Peterson Team Realty.2 
2 Because we have already determined that the decisions at issue would have a reasonably foreseeable material 
financial effect on your business interest in Peterson Team Realty, we do not further analyze those decisions' effect on 
your other interests.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Does the Act prohibit you from taking part in decisions relating to a land use ordinance that 
will determine the zone within the City in which commercial medical marijuana businesses will be 
permitted, given that you are a full-time real estate broker for Peterson Team Realty and that your 
real estate office is within 500 feet of the proposed medical marijuana zone?

CONCLUSION: Yes. The Act prohibits you from taking part in decisions relating to the land use ordinance 
because those decisions will have a reasonably foreseeable material financial effect on your 
business interest in Peterson Team Realty.
